title: 3 injured in Theater District shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Three men were injured in a shooting at 216 Tremont St. in Boston's Theater District on Monday night.
The victims sustained non-life-threatening injuries; police have not released their identities.
No arrests have been made; the Boston Police Department is investigating and requesting information from the public.

title: Healey vows urgent response as Haverhill sewage spill fouls Merrimack River
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Millions of gallons of untreated sewage spilled into Merrimack River after a wastewater main failure in Haverhill.
Governor Maura Healey announced efforts to install a temporary bypass pipe, aiming to restore wastewater flow quickly.
Beaches and shellfish areas in several Massachusetts towns remain closed due to the spill's impact on water quality.

title: Bruins ‘comfortable’ taking a leap of faith on two goalie prospects in 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins selected goalies Yuri Ivanov and Roberto Henriquez during the 2026 NHL Draft to bolster goaltending depth.
Ivanov was chosen second round (No. 56 overall) and will continue development in Russia, showcasing a .924 save percentage in junior league.
Roberto Henriquez, joining Boston College, impressed with a .921 save percentage for Green Bay Gamblers and praised for his competitiveness.

title: Extreme heat is gripping New England. An ER doctor shares how to stay safe.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Extreme heat and humidity grip New England, prompting emergency departments to prepare for heat-related illnesses.
Dr. Andrew Eyre advises quick cooling measures for heat stroke symptoms, including using ice packs and shade.
People are urged to limit sun exposure, stay hydrated, and check on vulnerable populations during the heat wave.

title: Market Basket named a top employer in latest U.S. News & World Report list
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Market Basket named a top workplace by U.S. News & World Report for 2026-2027 in the consumer products category.
The honor follows a power struggle resulting in former CEO Arthur T. Demoulas being ousted by majority-stakeholder siblings.
Market Basket President Chuck Casassa highlights company benefits like competitive pay and profit sharing for 32,000 employees.

title: Revolution still chasing permanent move for Matt Turner, possible Designated Player acquisition
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Chris Tierney named general manager and chief soccer officer of New England Revolution, replacing Curt Onalfo.
Revolution aims to keep goalkeeper Matt Turner beyond current loan from French club Lyon.
Team considering a Designated Player signing to enhance roster during MLS transfer window.

title: One charged with OUI after woman allegedly fell from the roof of a moving car in South Boston
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brandon Harper, 38, from Dorchester, was charged with OUI after a woman fell from his car roof in South Boston.
The woman, seriously injured, fell off the moving vehicle near Kosciuszko Circle and was treated at Tufts Medical Center.
Harper's breath test showed 0.275 and 0.247 BAC, far exceeding Massachusetts' legal limit of 0.08 for driving.

title: Boston magazine names Cambridge’s Fallow Kin the Best New Restaurant of 2026
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Fallow Kin in Cambridge named Best New Restaurant of 2026 by Boston magazine in the 52nd annual Best of Boston awards.
Located at 853 Main St., Fallow Kin opened last fall, focusing on sustainably sourced, regional, and vegetable-driven dishes.
The restaurant is a collaboration between Talulla's Conor Dennehy, Danielle Ayer, and Chef Marcos Sanchez, emphasizing food waste reduction.

title: Suspect flees after crowd throws drinks at lone BPD officer, prompting renewed staffing worries
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A lone Boston police officer was attacked with drinks and other items by a crowd while attempting to arrest a suspect.
The Boston Police Patrolmen’s Association called for increased staffing in light of the officer's assault by a large crowd in Dorchester.
Boston is facing police staffing issues with resignations and retirements, leading to increased overtime and fewer available officers.

title: What you need to know about Boston Harborfest
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston Harborfest returns July 2-4 with free activities, historical reenactments, and music across Boston's waterfront.
The 52nd Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, featuring concerts and a drone show, will occur on July 4 at the Hatch Memorial Shell.
Public transportation is recommended for Harborfest; MBTA offers free rides on July 4 post-8:30 p.m. due to limited parking.
